opp.name: Fulton Bank [S2P]
opp.next_step: Jul 02, 2026 (due Jul 02, 2026): Test - Next STep June 1 - Keith Woody is still working through the budgeting process.
opp.ais_why:
• Aug 2025 call invited Keith Woody to Zycus Horizon specifically around the launch of ANA (Autonomous Negotiating Agent), with the discussion explicitly aligning his negotiation expertise to the new agentic AI product.
• Notes state ANA integration into Zycus' AI-powered platform exemplifies negotiation-driven procurement efficiency; Keith offered as potential speaker on online ANA event, signaling executive-level AI affinity.
• Single recorded call but the AI subject matter was the central thesis; S2P scope definition planned 2026, implementation 2027. Confidence medium given one call and budget cycle delay.

contacts.name: Keith Woody
contacts.title: Senior Vice President, Chief Procurement and Real Estate Officer

items.quote: Every 2026 phone outreach logged 'Could Not Connect'; last real buyer response was Jason Miller's 25 Mar 2026 email.
Keith asked Zycus to help build a business case; no ROI or savings model exists.
No competitor named in any source; benchmarking implies a field that has not been disclosed.
items.source: Avoma call 8 Aug 2025 + SF Competitors__c blank

metrics.narrative: No quantified value case exists. On the 8 Aug 2025 call Keith Woody asked Zycus to help 'building a proper business case' and requested a 'benchmarking session to check what sort of value addition can be done by different vendors', and said he wanted peers 'similar to my asset size' for comparison. That confirms Fulton has not yet built an internal ROI, savings, cycle-time, or cost-per-invoice model. The qualitative driver is clear (a planned procure-to-pay transformation to modernize legacy processes) but the financial justification is unbuilt, and the buyer explicitly wants Zycus's help to build it. Quantifying the case with an asset-size peer bank is the move that would anchor budget.

champion.narrative: Keith Woody is a developing champion: senior (SVP/CPO), owns the S2P budget and timeline, engaged positively on the 8 Aug 2025 call with the relevant stakeholder references (ING Bank, Amerihealth, Carlson) and the ANA product, and agreed in principle to a benchmarking session. What has developed is genuine executive rapport and a stated openness to a Zycus-led business case. What is still missing is any active advocacy: he has not introduced Zycus to his team, shared internal requirements or timelines, driven a next step, or reconnected in 2026, and Jason Miller's 25 Mar 2026 'too early to reconnect' shows the wider account is cool. The relationship is single-threaded and passive, which is the deal's central risk.

competition.narrative: No competitor has been named in any source: Competitors__c is blank, the single Avoma call names no rival, and no task, next step, or field-history entry references competitive activity. The deal is genuinely pre-competitive rather than sole-sourced: Keith requested a benchmarking session against asset-size-peer banks, which signals a multi-vendor evaluation is planned for when Fulton starts formal selection, not that Zycus has won unopposed. The rep's reference to prior Fulton procurement contacts and an Oct 2024 S2P conversation suggests Zycus is one of several early conversations. The competitive field will crystallize when evaluation begins; directly asking Keith who else Fulton will benchmark is the move to surface it.

sources: SF Task 2-3 Apr 2026 (Chris Gracias email to Andreas Jones)
identify_pain.narrative: The pain is a legacy procure-to-pay estate that Fulton plans to transform, confirmed qualitatively by Keith on the 8 Aug 2025 call when he framed a phased S2P modernization ('very specific to procure to pay', scope definition 2026, implementation 2027). Zycus's outbound also probed BFSI-specific pain: Chris Gracias emailed Andreas Jones on 2-3 Apr 2026 about 'poor intake visibility means you find maverick spend after the fact' and vendor-governance risk. The pain is real and owner-anchored to Keith, but it is unquantified and not yet urgent enough for the buyer to accelerate. Converting the pain into a dated compelling event is the gap.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics are visible. There has been no pricing, MSA/SOW, legal, procurement, or InfoSec discussion because the deal sits in early budget-building with a stated 2027 implementation. Current contract expiration for any incumbent tool is unknown, so there is no forcing function on record. Paper-process visibility will only come once the buyer starts formal evaluation; asking Keith how Fulton procures and approves enterprise software during the benchmarking session would create the first visibility.

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Keith Woody, Senior Vice President, Chief Procurement and Real Estate Officer
economic_buyer.narrative: Keith Woody, SVP and Chief Procurement and Real Estate Officer, is the budget owner and controlled the timeline conversation on the 8 Aug 2025 call, stating 'we're in budget cycle today' and 'that's the current plan of record.' He carries clear procurement authority for an S2P purchase. However his sponsorship is passive and remote: the only substantive contact is that Aug 2025 call, the 2 Jul 2026 next step still has him 'working through the budgeting process', and every 2026 attempt to reach him failed to connect. The EB is identified but not actively engaged, and re-establishing a live conversation with Keith is the gating step.

decision_process.narrative: The buyer's process is only loosely mapped from one call: Keith described a phased internal plan (budget cycle now, scope definition 2026, implementation 2027) and deferred both vendor benchmarking and event attendance without committing dates. No RFI/RFP, shortlist, or approval chain has been surfaced, and no second buyer stakeholder has joined a call, so the committee and approval steps are unknown. The call sequence itself shows a single-threaded, buyer-paced process that has slowed. Establishing who besides Keith owns the S2P selection (Jason Miller and Andreas Jones are the likely sourcing/IT participants) is required to read the real process.

decision_criteria.narrative: No evaluation criteria have been documented or discussed. The only directional signal is Keith's request for a benchmarking session against banks of similar asset size, which implies peer-comparability and demonstrated BFSI value will matter, and his interest in the ANA agentic-negotiation product suggests AI capability is a criterion he personally weights. Nothing has been confirmed because formal evaluation has not started; the buyer said vendor evaluation would begin early 2026 and it has slipped. Running the benchmarking session and capturing Keith's stated requirements is how the criteria get defined.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.full: Losing on capability fit — AIS 7 (Moderate). Keith engaged directly with the ANA autonomous-negotiating-agent concept on the 8 Aug 2025 call and the products in scope include Merlin, Merlin Intake, Agentic AI, and ANA, but the AI appetite has not been re-tested in 2026.

Buyer is leaning elsewhere — The ANA agentic-negotiation story landed with Keith on the 8 Aug 2025 call, aligning to his negotiation expertise, but it was never followed up with a working demo or a Merlin/ANA briefing. The AI wedge is real and under-leveraged.

blocks.title: What moves it forward

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 14 is driven by Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 12 is carried by champion strength +0.30 (weight 15); exec access strength +0.30 (weight 15). Offsetting it: differentiation strength -0.40 (weight 20).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 52 (roughly flat): Next Step carries 2 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 4 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, stage integrity, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 0 is anchored on the stage win (12), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 52, and dragged by risk 14. Read is Partial Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

contributions.evidence: Next Step carries 2 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

items: Last buyer response of any kind: 25 Mar 2026 email ('too early to reconnect').
confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is cooling. One buyer call in 11 months, a March 2026 deferral, and a run of failed outbound calls indicate the buyer has gone quiet against its own stated timeline.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 12 Aug 2025, 325 days old, entire life in Qualified (~11 months). Close date moved 31 Aug 2026 -> 11 Dec 2026 on 30 Jun 2026, now 161 days out. Last genuine buyer engagement is the 8 Aug 2025 call plus a 25 Mar 2026 brush-off email from Jason Miller ('too early to reconnect'); every other 2026 touch is a failed outbound 'Could Not Connect'. The buyer's own stated early-2026 benchmarking window has passed with no session booked, so momentum has thinned. This is a 12-15 month enterprise S2P cycle with a stated 2027 implementation, so a Dec 2026 close remains premature, but the primary problem now is thinning engagement, not just the date.
evidence: Keith Woody (SVP, Chief Procurement and Real Estate Officer) stated on the 8 Aug 2025 call: 'we're in budget cycle today, and we think towards end of year, it's more scope definition in '26, and again, it's very specific to procure to pay. We're in the scope definition in '26, and we look to have implementation in '27. That's the current plan of record.'

Keith agreed benchmarking timing on the same call: 'beginning next year would be the right timing for something like that. Yes.' That early-2026 window has now passed with no benchmarking session booked.

Jason Miller (VP Strategic Sourcing Manager) replied by email on 25 Mar 2026: 'No changes to report. Too early to reconnect at this point, but appreciate the follow up.' This is the most recent real buyer response and it is a soft deferral.

Every 2026 phone outreach to Keith Woody, Jason Miller, and Andreas Jones is logged 'Could Not Connect' (tasks 30 Apr, 6 May, 22 May, 27 May, 28 May, 1 Jun, 9 Jun, 12 Jun, 25 Jun 2026) - no live buyer conversation reconnected.

Close date was pushed by Edward Dlugosz from 31 Aug 2026 to 11 Dec 2026 on 30 Jun 2026, and Division moved Zycus -> New Markets the same day; the date is now less premature but still ahead of the buyer's 2027 implementation plan.
Only one buyer call exists in 11 months and no Economic-Buyer/pricing/negotiation/contract activity is logged; this is early discovery and budget-building, not a deal near signature.

Products scope is a full S2P + AI suite (iContract, eProcurement, eInvoicing, Merlin, Merlin Intake, Agentic AI, ANA) at a $110k booking, signalling a first-slice or pilot value, not the transformation contract.
north_star_verdict.headline: Developing SVP champion Keith Woody, but the buyer has gone quiet since the 8 Aug 2025 call. Every 2026 outreach attempt failed to connect, Jason Miller said 'too early to reconnect' on 25 Mar 2026, and the promised early-2026 benchmarking session never happened. Close date was pushed to 11 Dec 2026 but still runs ahead of the buyer's stated 2027 implementation plan.

flags: Multi-thread now: Keith Woody has gone quiet, so engage the relevant stakeholder and Andreas Jones directly with a BFSI-specific proof point before the benchmarking session.
The buyer's own early-2026 benchmarking window has passed and Jason Miller said 'too early to reconnect' on 25 Mar 2026; re-establish a dated reason to meet or the deal drifts to 2027.
No quantified business case exists; convert Keith's request into a scheduled peer-bank value session to build urgency and defend budget.
best_practice_check.summary: Single-threaded on a quiet SVP with no quantified case; the deal will drift to 2027 with no Zycus mindshare unless the team reconnects the buyer and books the promised value session.

Zycus is positioned as an early relationship-builder with BFSI proof points (ING Bank, Amerihealth, Carlson) and AI/ANA differentiation. The competitive field will form once Fulton starts formal evaluation; until then the field is genuinely unknown, not sole-sourced.

Zycus fits on BFSI references and AI, but nothing has been validated because formal evaluation has not begun.
